caseFlag.title: Operator user_042: 15 kosmetische Defekte in PO-00012/18/24
caseFlag.detail: Operator user_042 verursachte 15 kosmetische Defekte (LABEL_MISALIGN: 11, VISUAL_SCRATCH: 4) in Fertigungsaufträgen PO-00012, PO-00018 und PO-00024 – hauptsächlich in SEC-00001 (54 Installationen). Schulungsmaßnahme empfohlen.

caseFlag.title: Bediener user_042 – Kosmetische Defekte (LABEL_MISALIGN / VISUAL_SCRATCH)
caseFlag.detail: user_042 verursachte in PO-00012 (5 Defekte), PO-00018 (5 Defekte) und PO-00024 (5 Defekte) insgesamt 15 kosmetische Defekte (LABEL_MISALIGN + VISUAL_SCRATCH). Hauptaktivität in SEC-00004 (48 Installationen). Schulung / Einweisung empfohlen.

fieldClaims.complaintText: Multiple MC-200 failures. The units are failing after thermal cycling in the enclosure. C12 (100uF) seems to be the point of failure every time. We noticed the ElektroParts logo on the caps. We need a solution before we ship the next 50 units.

System outage reported at the client site. MC-200 unit stopped working out of nowhere. No error codes, just no power. Opened it up and C12 looks scorched. We suspect the ElektroParts components are the culprit here. Pls advise on RMA process.

The firmware version on the new units is out of date. We were promised v2.1 but received v1.9. We now have to manually update each unit before installation. This is a waste of our time. Please ensure the correct firmware is loaded next time.

MC-200 units are not holding their calibration. After 2-3 months, the R33 resistor drifts and the unit becomes unstable. This is especially noticeable at higher temperatures. Performance is not meeting spec. What are our options for repair?
Instruction manual is missing from the box. We have new techs who need the wiring diagram. Can you send a PDF or physical copies? This is basic stuff that should be in every box. Thanks.

MC-200 units are failing after 8 weeks. Symptoms include intermittent instability and reduced performance. We suspect R33 thermal drift is the root cause. The units behave differently when they are hot vs cold. This is unacceptable for our application.

Severe failure rates on the MC-200 line. Units are blacking out after thermal cycles. The C12 cap (100uF) is clearly the weak link. We believe the ElektroParts supply is defective. We cannot accept any more units with these components installed.

We are experiencing a total failure on three MC-200 units installed last month. The system just went dark during a standard thermal cycle test. It seems the C12 capacitor (100uF) is leaking or shorted. This is the second time this week. Please investigate the ElektroParts batch immediately.

Our field service team is reporting multiple MC-200 outages. The units die abruptly after a couple of weeks. Looks like the C12 100uF capacitor from ElektroParts is failing due to heat cycling. We need a formal Root Cause Analysis for this supplier issue.

Intermittent disconnects on the RS485 port. The connector feels loose on several units. We have to wiggle the cable to get a signal. This is not acceptable for an industrial environment. Please advise on a fix.

The enclosure color is different from the last order. These are supposed to be light gray, but they are dark gray. It doesn't match our existing equipment. Why did the color change? Please confirm the spec.

MC-200 unit dead on arrival after 2 weeks in field. Sudden outage during normal operation. The C12 cap appears to have failed under thermal stress. Are you guys still using ElektroParts for the 100uF capacitors? We had better luck with the previous supplier.

The enclosure color is different from the last order. These are supposed to be light gray, but they are dark gray. It doesn't match our existing equipment. Why did the color change? Please confirm the spec.

MC-200 performance is degrading over time. After 3 months, the units are not as precise. We suspect R33 thermal drift is causing the instability. It seems to get worse as the unit heats up. Can we swap these out for high-precision resistors?

Power supply terminal block is stripped on one unit. We can't tighten the wire down. It seems the screw was over-torqued at the factory. Please send a replacement unit or a new terminal block. This is a quality control issue.

MC-200 units failing after 200 hours of use. Symptoms: sudden outage, no LED activity. Inspection shows C12 (100uF) capacitor has failed. These ElektroParts components are not suitable for our thermal environment. Please send replacements with a different cap brand.

Found a pattern of failures in the MC-200 series. Units run fine for 15 days then die after the temp cycling phase. Its always the C12 100uF capacitor. Is there a known issue with the supplier ElektroParts? This is causing major downtime for us.

Power supply terminal block is stripped on one unit. We can't tighten the wire down. It seems the screw was over-torqued at the factory. Please send a replacement unit or a new terminal block. This is a quality control issue.

Unit is blowing the internal fuse as soon as it's powered on. We've checked our wiring and it's correct. It seems like a short circuit inside the unit. This is the 3rd unit this week. Please investigate.

Customer is complaining about MC-200 units failing early. They run for a few weeks and then just stop. Initial teardown shows C12 is dead. This 100uF part from ElektroParts isnt holding up to thermal cycling. What is the plan for remediation?

Urgent issue with MC-200 motor controllers. After about 3 weeks of operation, units are losing power completely. Our tech found that the 100uF cap at C12 is blown. Are these parts from ElektroParts? We need replacements that dont fail during heat cycles.

Having issues with MC-200 controllers. 4 units failed this morning after the overnight thermal cycle. Total power loss. Our engineer points to C12 (100uF) failure. This ElektroParts batch is looking very suspicious. Need an ETA on fixed units.

MC-200 units are showing gradual performance loss. After about 12 weeks, the R33 resistor starts to drift. This causes intermittent instability in the motor control. We need units that can handle the thermal load without drifting. Please respond.

MC-200 failure report. Unit failed during thermal cycle test. Investigation revealed C12 cap (100uF) is open circuit. Supplier marked as ElektroParts. This is the 5th unit this month. Please expedite the warranty claim.

The LED display on the sensor unit is flickering. It's hard to read the values. This is happening on multiple units in the same batch. We suspect a loose connection or a bad driver. Please look into this.

Missing mounting screws in the package. 10 units arrived without the hardware kit. We can't install them without the screws. This is delaying our project. Please send the missing hardware via overnight shipping.

Intermittent disconnects on the RS485 port. The connector feels loose on several units. We have to wiggle the cable to get a signal. This is not acceptable for an industrial environment. Please advise on a fix.

Customer reports MC-200 instability. The unit works fine for the first 2 months, then starts acting erratic. We believe R33 resistor is the cause of this thermal drift. Performance is significantly reduced when the cabinet gets warm. We need a fix ASAP.

The fan on the motor controller is making a loud grinding noise. It sounds like a bearing failure. We've had 2 units with this issue in the first week. Is this a common problem? We need quiet and reliable fans.

Software interface is crashing when we try to save settings. This is happening on all the new units. We need a patch or a workaround. Our production is stalled until we can configure these controllers.

Missing mounting screws in the package. 10 units arrived without the hardware kit. We can't install them without the screws. This is delaying our project. Please send the missing hardware via overnight shipping.

The housing on the latest batch of motor controllers is cracked. It looks like the plastic is too brittle. We found 3 units with damage right out of the box. Please send replacements and check your QC process.

Unit is blowing the internal fuse as soon as it's powered on. We've checked our wiring and it's correct. It seems like a short circuit inside the unit. This is the 3rd unit this week. Please investigate.

Wrong labels on the sensor units. The part numbers don't match the documentation. This is causing confusion in our warehouse. Please send the correct labels or RMA the whole batch. This is a simple mistake that should have been caught.

Wrong labels on the sensor units. The part numbers don't match the documentation. This is causing confusion in our warehouse. Please send the correct labels or RMA the whole batch. This is a simple mistake that should have been caught.

The housing on the latest batch of motor controllers is cracked. It looks like the plastic is too brittle. We found 3 units with damage right out of the box. Please send replacements and check your QC process.

Software interface is crashing when we try to save settings. This is happening on all the new units. We need a patch or a workaround. Our production is stalled until we can configure these controllers.

The fan on the motor controller is making a loud grinding noise. It sounds like a bearing failure. We've had 2 units with this issue in the first week. Is this a common problem? We need quiet and reliable fans.
The RJ45 port is misaligned with the housing. We can't plug in the ethernet cable. It looks like the PCB is not seated correctly. We need to return these units for repair. Please send the RMA info.
The RJ45 port is misaligned with the housing. We can't plug in the ethernet cable. It looks like the PCB is not seated correctly. We need to return these units for repair. Please send the RMA info.

atRiskProducts.reasonDetail: R33 (PM-00015, 4,7k Thermal) Thermodrift – Feldbeschwerde FC-00015 nach 70 Tagen (DE). Design-issue BN-00004.
R33/PM-00015 (Widerstand 4.7kΩ, BN-00004) Thermaldrift nach 8–12 Wochen. Produkt im kritischen Altersfenster – proaktive Kundeninformation empfohlen.
R33 (PM-00015, 4,7k Thermal) Thermodrift – Feldbeschwerde FC-00017 nach 56 Tagen (ES). Design-issue BN-00004.
R33 (PM-00015, 4,7k Thermal) Thermodrift – Feldbeschwerde FC-00016 nach 63 Tagen (PL). Design-issue BN-00004.
R33 (PM-00015, 4,7k Thermal) Thermodrift – Feldbeschwerde FC-00013 nach 56 Tagen (DE). Design-issue BN-00004.
PM-00008/C12 Lieferantendefekt (ElektroParts SB-00009) – Build-Datum im Risikobereich; Latente Population ohne Feldklaim aber im kritischen Altersfenster
Kondensator C12 (PM-00008, 100uF X7R) aus ElektroParts-Charge SB-00009 – Feldbeschwerde FC-00004 (C12 verbrannt, 49 Tage nach Bau, US).
Kondensator C12 (PM-00008, 100uF X7R) aus ElektroParts-Charge SB-00009 – Feldbeschwerde FC-00012 (C12 versagt nach Thermozyklus, 49 Tage, US).

investigations.title: Feldausfälle MC-200: Kondensator 100uF X7R (C12) – ElektroParts-Chargen SB-00008/09/10
investigations.summary: Chargen SB-00009 (28 Defekte), SB-00010 (5 Defekte) und SB-00008 (1 Defekt) des Lieferanten ElektroParts GmbH für Bauteil PM-00008 (Kondensator 100uF X7R, Referenz C12) sind mit SOLDER_COLD-Defekten belastet. 14 Feldbeschwerden aus DE, FR, IT, US berichten von Totalausfall nach 21–56 Tagen, konsistent auf C12-Kapazitätsversagen nach Thermozyklen. 25 betroffene Produkte in SB-00009, gesamt 36 über alle drei Chargen. BOM-Komponente BN-00003/ART-00001 als supply-issue markiert.
investigations.rootCause: Defekte Kondensatoren 100uF X7R (PM-00008) aus ElektroParts-Chargen SB-00008/09/10 – Kaltlötstellen (SOLDER_COLD) führen zu Kapazitätsverlust und Totalausfall unter Thermostress

timeline.event: FC-00008: C12 open-circuit bestätigt – 5. Einheit diesen Monat
FC-00003/FC-00007: Muster bestätigt, Chargen unter Verdacht
Qualitäts-Co-Pilot: Chargen als bad markiert, Untersuchung eröffnet

suggestedActions: Sofortiger Lieferstopp für ElektroParts PM-00008 aus Chargen SB-00008, SB-00009, SB-00010
Alle betroffenen Produkte (PRD-00001 bis PRD-00030, PRD-00048, PRD-00076, PRD-00078, PRD-00099, PRD-00169) auf Feldrückruf prüfen
Ersatzbeschaffung PM-00008 bei alternativem Lieferanten einleiten
Formelle 8D-Analyse mit ElektroParts GmbH anstoßen – Fokus SOLDER_COLD-Ursache
Alle noch auf Lager befindlichen Einheiten aus SB-00009 sperren und 100%-Prüfung anordnen

investigations.title: Prozessdrift Montage Linie 1 (SEC-00001): VIB_FAIL-Spike KW49–52/2025 – Drehmomentschlüssel
investigations.summary: Sektion SEC-00001 (Montage Linie 1, fac-aug) zeigte in KW49–52/2025 einen Anstieg von VIB_FAIL-Defekten: 4 (KW49), 6 (KW50), 4 (KW51), 6 (KW52) – insgesamt 20 Defekte. Ursache: vermutlich falsch kalibrierter Drehmomentschlüssel. Der Spike ist seit Ende 2025 nicht mehr beobachtet worden und gilt als selbstkorrigiert. Kalibriernachweis und Korrekturmaßnahme sind noch zu dokumentieren.
investigations.rootCause: Prozessdrift in SEC-00001 (Montage Linie 1): Drehmomentschlüssel außerhalb Kalibriertoleranz führte zu VIB_FAIL-Defekten in KW49–52/2025 (gesamt 20 Defekte). Seit KW01/2026 keine weiteren Defekte – wahrscheinlich selbstkorrigiert

timeline.event: Keine weiteren VIB_FAIL-Defekte in SEC-00001 – Spike selbstkorrigiert

suggestedActions: Kalibrierprotokoll des Drehmomentschlüssels für KW49–52/2025 rückwirkend prüfen und dokumentieren
Nachkalibrierung aller Drehmomentschlüssel in SEC-00001 mit Prüfnachweis bestätigen
Präventiv: wöchentliche Kalibrierprüfung für Drehmomentschlüssel auf Linie 1 einführen
Betroffene Produkte aus KW49–52/2025 auf VIB_FAIL-Risiko stichprobenartig prüfen

investigations.title: Designfehler ART-00001: R33-Widerstand PM-00015 (4,7k Thermal) – Thermodrift im Feld
investigations.summary: 5 Feldbeschwerden (FC-00016, FC-00015, FC-00017, FC-00013 + weitere) zu ART-00001 berichten von Instabilität und Leistungsabfall nach 56–70 Tagen, konsistent auf R33 (PM-00015, Widerstand 4,7k Thermal) zurückgeführt. Es existieren keine entsprechenden Fabrikdefekte – klares Designproblem. Betroffene Märkte: DE, PL, ES. Build-Alter bei Beschwerde: 56–70 Tage (8–10 Wochen). BOM-Komponente BN-00004/ART-00001 als design-issue markiert. Latente Population in der 8–12-Wochen-Altersgruppe proaktiv gefährdet.
investigations.rootCause: Designproblem: R33 (PM-00015, Widerstand 4,7k Thermal) in ART-00001 zeigt unzureichende Thermostabilität. Nach 8–10 Wochen Feldbetrieb führt Thermodrift zu Instabilität und Leistungsabfall – ohne Fabrikdetektierbarkeit (kein Fabrikdefekt registriert)

timeline.event: FC-00016: Erste Beschwerde R33-Thermodrift (PL, PRD-00084, 63 Tage nach Bau)
Qualitäts-Co-Pilot: Design-Flag BN-00004 gesetzt, Gefährdungsprodukte identifiziert

suggestedActions: R33 (PM-00015) durch hochpräzisen Widerstand mit niedrigem Temperaturkoeffizient (≤25ppm/°C) ersetzen – Design-ECO einleiten
Alle ART-00001-Einheiten im Feldalter 8–12 Wochen proaktiv kontaktieren und Tausch anbieten
Thermische Qualifikationstests für PM-00015 nachfordern – Lieferant TechSupply Europe einbinden
Screening-Test bei Fertigung bis zur Design-Korrektur: Thermozyklus für ART-00001 ergänzen
Feldbericht an TechSupply Europe (SB-00017, 34 Defekte) zur Qualitätsbewertung senden

investigations.title: Kosmetische Defekte Operator user_042: LABEL_MISALIGN / VISUAL_SCRATCH (PO-00012/18/24)
investigations.summary: Operator user_042 verursachte in drei Fertigungsaufträgen (PO-00012, PO-00018, PO-00024) insgesamt 15 kosmetische Defekte: 11× LABEL_MISALIGN und 4× VISUAL_SCRATCH. Der Hauptarbeitsbereich ist SEC-00001 (Montage Linie 1, fac-aug, 54 Installationen). Kein funktionaler Einfluss; keine Feldbeschwerden. Schulungsbedarf identifiziert. Zum Vergleich: user_017 verursachte lediglich 1 Defekt (LABEL_MISALIGN, PO-00023).
investigations.rootCause: Operatorfehler user_042 in SEC-00001 (Montage Linie 1): 11 LABEL_MISALIGN- und 4 VISUAL_SCRATCH-Defekte über PO-00012/18/24 – kosmetisch, kein Sicherheitsrisiko

timeline.event: Qualitäts-Co-Pilot: Operatormuster erkannt, SEC-00001 markiert, Schulung empfohlen

suggestedActions: Einzelgespräch mit Operator user_042 – Schwerpunkt korrekte Etikettierung und kratzervermeidende Handhabung
Nachschulung user_042: Visuelle Qualitätsstandards Montage Linie 1
Vergleich mit user_017 als Best-Practice-Referenz (nur 1 Defekt)
Kurzfristige Stichprobenprüfung aller von user_042 bearbeiteten Einheiten in PO-00012/18/24
